revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building |名称= 朝熊神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Asakuma-jinja02.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字櫻木2566-1 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 15.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 10.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[年神|大歳神]]
苔虫神
朝熊水神 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 小朝熊、朝熊社、小朝熊神社、さくらの宮 |札所等= |例祭= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月19日]]） |神事= [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.4875|N|136.75306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Toshigami|qid=Q3028663|1=Toshigami}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} is an {{ill|setsumatsusha|lt=auxiliary shrine (sessha)|ja|末社|lt=Sessha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/setsumatsusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} at {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It holds the highest Rank among the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrinesIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 108. This article also covers {{ill|Asakuma Gozen Shrine|ja|朝熊御前神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Gozen Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朝熊神社 (伊勢市)|1=Asakuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17191186}}, which shares the same grounds. Asakuma Shrine is a highly prestigious shrine within the Inner Shrine network and hosts more {{ill|Shinto rituals|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}} than other auxiliary shrines. == Overview == Located at 2566-1 Sakura-gi, Asakuma-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 188, the shrine sits on a hill east of the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|qid=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}} and Asakuma RiverTanigawa (1986): p. 45. Across the Asakuma River lies {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine (Naiku)|lt=Kagami-no-Miya Shrine (Naiku)|lt=Kagami-no-Miya|ja|鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kagami-no-Miya Shrine (Naiku)|lt=Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine (Naiku)|qq=}}. Both shrines have deep ties with the Isuzu RiverGakken Publishing (2013): p. 60. Upon entering the grounds, Asakuma Shrine is on the right (east), and Asakuma Gozen Shrine is on the left (west). The proper order of worship is to visit Asakuma Shrine first, followed by Asakuma Gozen Shrine. The shrine grounds cover approximately 5,002 m²Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 9. Like many auxiliary shrines, no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qid=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} is present. Both shrine buildings face southwest. == Asakuma Shrine == Asakuma Shrine holds the top Rank among the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrines and receives ceremonial treatment akin to the {{ill|betsugu|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/betsugu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines. Similarly, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, the highest auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine), enjoys special privilegesSakurai (1969): p. 91. The name "Asakuma" (朝熊) is believed to derive from "Asakuma" (浅隈), meaning a "shallow meandering river". Other theories suggest it refers to a bear appearing during {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}'s ascetic training or relates to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}'s alternative name "Ashizu-hime". The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} architectural style, with a wooden shingle roof, surrounded by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (sacred fence)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 193. There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=Torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q234731}}. === Enshrined Deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, Koke-mushi-no-Kami (苔虫神), and Asakuma Mizu-no-Kami (朝熊水神). These three deities are considered the {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of the Asakuma plain, associated with {{ill|Five Grains|en|Five Grains|qid=Q1191138|lt=agriculture|1=Five Grains}} and {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|qid=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}. == Asakuma Gozen Shrine == Ranked second among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11, its structure is identical to Asakuma Shrine's. The enshrined deity is Asakuma Gozen-no-Kami (朝熊御前神). Initially, {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine (Naiku)|lt=Kagami-no-Miya Shrine (Naiku)|lt=Kagami-no-Miya|ja|鏡宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya|qq=}} served as the front shrine (御前社) for Asakuma Shrine when it was rebuilt in 1663Tanigawa (1986): p. 51. Later, Asakuma Gozen Shrine was constructed next to Asakuma Shrine, with Kagami-no-Miya becoming an independent shrine. == History == According to the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} established Asakuma Shrine in the 27th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}'s reign to enshrine a stone manifestation of Ōtoshi-no-Kami. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was founded before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. Records indicate that during the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|qid=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}, rice tributes (役夫工米) were levied for shrine construction. Although reconstruction records ceased afterward, rituals continued until the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}Tanigawa (1986): pp. 46–47. Thanks to the efforts of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga), the shrine was restored in July 1663. == Rituals == Rituals comparable to those held at {{ill|betsugu|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/betsugu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are performed. Special ceremonies like the {{ill|Yuki no Ōmike no Gi|ja|由貴大御饌の儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki no Ōmike no Gi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} take place during the {{ill|Sansetsusai|ja|三節祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansetsusai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} festivals. == Cherry Blossoms of Asakuma Shrine == Asakuma Shrine is renowned for its cherry blossoms. The poet {{ill|Saigyō|en|Saigyō|qid=Q224689|lt=Saigyō|1=Saigyō}} composed the following tanka in the {{ill|Shokukokin Wakashū|en|Shokukokin Wakashū|qid=Q1143045|lt=Shokukokin Wakashū|1=Shokukokin Wakashū}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 192: {{Cquote|Calmly I entrust my heart
To the divine winds,
As the cherry blossoms bloom
In the shrine of Sakura.|qq=}} == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884857}}. * Accessible by bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Iseshi Station|1=Iseshi Station}} to Asakuma Town. == Transportation Access == ;公共交通 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]]{{ill|Toba Line|en|Toba Line|qid=Q5357612|lt=鳥羽線|1=Toba Line}} ** [[朝熊駅]]より徒歩約16分（約1.4km）。 ** [[五十鈴川駅]]より[[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「[[夫婦岩]]東口」行き乗車、「朝熊インター」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約4分（約370m）。ただし本数が少ない。 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[五十鈴ヶ丘駅]]より徒歩約27分（約2.2km）。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン]][[朝熊インターチェンジ|朝熊IC]]より約4分（約600m）。 == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和44th year 5月20日、251p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|qid=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々―神社と聖地 第六巻 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊』[[白水社]]、1986th year 3月31日、492p. ISBN 4-560-02216-X == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|en|Saminaga Shrine|qid=Q17209205|lt=Saminaga Shrine|1=Saminaga Shrine}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Asakuma-jinja|qq=}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0303.html 朝熊神社（あさくまじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0304.html 朝熊御前神社（あさくまみまえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|朝熊神社 (伊勢市)|version=77002003|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Asakuma-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Asakuma Shrine (Q17191186)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|qid=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|lt=Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社 (伊勢市)|commons|Category:Asakuma-jinja|qid=Q17191186|1=Asakuma Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|qid=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.487512|136.753111|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181115 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17191186}} [[da:d:Q17191186]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]]==Notes== [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 200+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 250+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 300+ untranslated japanese characters]]
